10:     channel::{self, Receiver, Sender},
11:     context::Context,
12:     err::{
13:         ReadyTimeoutError, RecvError, SelectTimeoutError, SendError, TryReadyError, TrySelectError,
14:     },
15:     flavors, utils,
16: };
17: 
18: /// Temporary data that gets initialized during select or a blocking operation, and is consumed by
19: /// `read` or `write`.
20: ///
21: /// Each field contains data associated with a specific channel flavor.
22: // This is a private API that is used by the select macro.
23: #[derive(Debug, Default)]
24: pub struct Token {
25:     pub(crate) at: flavors::at::AtToken,
26:     pub(crate) array: flavors::array::ArrayToken,
27:     pub(crate) list: flavors::list::ListToken,
28:     #[allow(dead_code)]
29:     pub(crate) never: flavors::never::NeverToken,
30:     pub(crate) tick: flavors::tick::TickToken,
31:     pub(crate) zero: flavors::zero::ZeroToken,
32: }
33: 
34: /// Identifier associated with an operation by a specific thread on a specific channel.
35: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
36: pub struct Operation(usize);
37: 
38: impl Operation {
39:     /// Creates an operation identifier from a mutable reference.
40:     ///
41:     /// This function essentially just turns the address of the reference into a number. The
42:     /// reference should point to a variable that is specific to the thread and the operation,
43:     /// and is alive for the entire duration of select or blocking operation.
44:     #[inline]
45:     pub fn hook<T>(r: &mut T) -> Self {
46:         let val = r as *mut T as usize;
47:         // Make sure that the pointer address doesn't equal the numerical representation of
48:         // `Selected::{Waiting, Aborted, Disconnected}`.
49:         assert!(val > 2);
50:         Self(val)
51:     }
52: }
53: 
54: /// Current state of a select or a blocking operation.
55: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
56: pub enum Selected {
57:     /// Still waiting for an operation.
58:     Waiting,
59: 
60:     /// The attempt to block the current thread has been aborted.
61:     Aborted,
62: 
63:     /// An operation became ready because a channel is disconnected.
64:     Disconnected,
65: 
66:     /// An operation became ready because a message can be sent or received.
67:     Operation(Operation),
68: }
69: 
70: impl From<usize> for Selected {
71:     #[inline]
72:     fn from(val: usize) -> Self {
73:         match val {
74:             0 => Self::Waiting,
75:             1 => Self::Aborted,
76:             2 => Self::Disconnected,
77:             oper => Self::Operation(Operation(oper)),
78:         }
79:     }
80: }
81: 
82: impl From<Selected> for usize {
83:     #[inline]
84:     fn from(val: Selected) -> Self {
85:         match val {
86:             Selected::Waiting => 0,
87:             Selected::Aborted => 1,
88:             Selected::Disconnected => 2,
89:             Selected::Operation(Operation(val)) => val,
90:         }
91:     }
92: }
93: 
94: /// A receiver or a sender that can participate in select.
95: ///
96: /// This is a handle that assists select in executing an operation, registration, deciding on the
97: /// appropriate deadline for blocking, etc.
98: // This is a private API (exposed inside crossbeam_channel::internal module) that is used by the select macro.
99: pub trait SelectHandle {
100:     /// Attempts to select an operation and returns `true` on success.
101:     fn try_select(&self, token: &mut Token) -> bool;
102: 
103:     /// Returns a deadline for an operation, if there is one.
104:     fn deadline(&self) -> Option<Instant>;
105: 
106:     /// Registers an operation for execution and returns `true` if it is now ready.
107:     fn register(&self, oper: Operation, cx: &Context) -> bool;
108: 
109:     /// Unregisters an operation for execution.
110:     fn unregister(&self, oper: Operation);
111: 
112:     /// Attempts to select an operation the thread got woken up for and returns `true` on success.
113:     fn accept(&self, token: &mut Token, cx: &Context) -> bool;
114: 
115:     /// Returns `true` if an operation can be executed without blocking.
116:     fn is_ready(&self) -> bool;
117: 
118:     /// Registers an operation for readiness notification and returns `true` if it is now ready.
119:     fn watch(&self, oper: Operation, cx: &Context) -> bool;
120: 
121:     /// Unregisters an operation for readiness notification.
122:     fn unwatch(&self, oper: Operation);
123: }
124: 
125: impl<T: SelectHandle> SelectHandle for &T {
126:     fn try_select(&self, token: &mut Token) -> bool {
127:         (**self).try_select(token)
128:     }
129: 
130:     fn deadline(&self) -> Option<Instant> {
131:         (**self).deadline()
132:     }
133: 
134:     fn register(&self, oper: Operation, cx: &Context) -> bool {
135:         (**self).register(oper, cx)
136:     }
137: 
138:     fn unregister(&self, oper: Operation) {
139:         (**self).unregister(oper);
140:     }
141: 
142:     fn accept(&self, token: &mut Token, cx: &Context) -> bool {
143:         (**self).accept(token, cx)
144:     }
145: 
146:     fn is_ready(&self) -> bool {
147:         (**self).is_ready()
148:     }
149: 
150:     fn watch(&self, oper: Operation, cx: &Context) -> bool {
151:         (**self).watch(oper, cx)
152:     }
153: 
154:     fn unwatch(&self, oper: Operation) {
155:         (**self).unwatch(oper)
156:     }
157: }

159: /// Determines when a select operation should time out.
160: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Eq, PartialEq)]
161: enum Timeout {
162:     /// No blocking.
163:     Now,
164: 
165:     /// Block forever.
166:     Never,
167: 
168:     /// Time out after the time instant.
169:     At(Instant),
170: }
171: 
172: /// Runs until one of the operations is selected, potentially blocking the current thread.
173: ///
174: /// Successful receive operations will have to be followed up by `channel::read()` and successful
175: /// send operations by `channel::write()`.
176: fn run_select(
177:     handles: &mut [(&dyn SelectHandle, usize, *const u8)],
178:     timeout: Timeout,
179:     is_biased: bool,
180: ) -> Option<(Token, usize, *const u8)> {
181:     if handles.is_empty() {
182:         // Wait until the timeout and return.
183:         match timeout {
184:             Timeout::Now => return None,
185:             Timeout::Never => {
186:                 utils::sleep_until(None);
187:                 unreachable!();
188:             }
189:             Timeout::At(when) => {
190:                 utils::sleep_until(Some(when));
191:                 return None;
192:             }
193:         }
194:     }
195: 
196:     if !is_biased {
197:         // Shuffle the operations for fairness.
198:         utils::shuffle(handles);
199:     }
200: 
201:     // Create a token, which serves as a temporary variable that gets initialized in this function
202:     // and is later used by a call to `channel::read()` or `channel::write()` that completes the
203:     // selected operation.
204:     let mut token = Token::default();
205: 
206:     // Try selecting one of the operations without blocking.
207:     for &(handle, i, ptr) in handles.iter() {
208:         if handle.try_select(&mut token) {
209:             return Some((token, i, ptr));
210:         }
211:     }
212: 
213:     loop {
214:         // Prepare for blocking.
215:         let res = Context::with(|cx| {
216:             let mut sel = Selected::Waiting;
217:             let mut registered_count = 0;
218:             let mut index_ready = None;
219: 
220:             if let Timeout::Now = timeout {
221:                 cx.try_select(Selected::Aborted).unwrap();
222:             }
223: 
224:             // Register all operations.
225:             for (handle, i, _) in handles.iter_mut() {
226:                 registered_count += 1;
227: 
228:                 // If registration returns `false`, that means the operation has just become ready.
229:                 if handle.register(Operation::hook::<&dyn SelectHandle>(handle), cx) {
230:                     // Try aborting select.
231:                     sel = match cx.try_select(Selected::Aborted) {
232:                         Ok(()) => {
233:                             index_ready = Some(*i);
234:                             Selected::Aborted
235:                         }
236:                         Err(s) => s,
237:                     };
238:                     break;
239:                 }
240: 
241:                 // If another thread has already selected one of the operations, stop registration.
242:                 sel = cx.selected();
243:                 if sel != Selected::Waiting {
244:                     break;
245:                 }
246:             }
247: 
248:             if sel == Selected::Waiting {
249:                 // Check with each operation for how long we're allowed to block, and compute the
250:                 // earliest deadline.
251:                 let mut deadline: Option<Instant> = match timeout {
252:                     Timeout::Now => return None,
253:                     Timeout::Never => None,
254:                     Timeout::At(when) => Some(when),
255:                 };
256:                 for &(handle, _, _) in handles.iter() {
257:                     if let Some(x) = handle.deadline() {
258:                         deadline = deadline.map(|y| x.min(y)).or(Some(x));
259:                     }
260:                 }
261: 
262:                 // Block the current thread.
263:                 sel = cx.wait_until(deadline);
264:             }
265: 
266:             // Unregister all registered operations.
267:             for (handle, _, _) in handles.iter_mut().take(registered_count) {
268:                 handle.unregister(Operation::hook::<&dyn SelectHandle>(handle));
269:             }
270: 
271:             match sel {
272:                 Selected::Waiting => unreachable!(),
273:                 Selected::Aborted => {
274:                     // If an operation became ready during registration, try selecting it.
275:                     if let Some(index_ready) = index_ready {
276:                         for &(handle, i, ptr) in handles.iter() {
277:                             if i == index_ready && handle.try_select(&mut token) {
278:                                 return Some((i, ptr));
279:                             }
280:                         }
281:                     }
282:                 }
283:                 Selected::Disconnected => {}
284:                 Selected::Operation(_) => {
285:                     // Find the selected operation.
286:                     for (handle, i, ptr) in handles.iter_mut() {
287:                         // Is this the selected operation?
288:                         if sel == Selected::Operation(Operation::hook::<&dyn SelectHandle>(handle))
289:                         {
290:                             // Try selecting this operation.
291:                             if handle.accept(&mut token, cx) {
292:                                 return Some((*i, *ptr));
293:                             }
294:                         }
295:                     }
296:                 }
297:             }
298: 
299:             None
300:         });
301: 
302:         // Return if an operation was selected.
303:         if let Some((i, ptr)) = res {
304:             return Some((token, i, ptr));
305:         }
306: 
307:         // Try selecting one of the operations without blocking.
308:         for &(handle, i, ptr) in handles.iter() {
309:             if handle.try_select(&mut token) {
310:                 return Some((token, i, ptr));
311:             }
312:         }
313: 
314:         match timeout {
315:             Timeout::Now => return None,
316:             Timeout::Never => {}
317:             Timeout::At(when) => {
318:                 if Instant::now() >= when {
319:                     return None;
320:                 }
321:             }
322:         }
323:     }
324: }
325: 
326: /// Runs until one of the operations becomes ready, potentially blocking the current thread.
327: fn run_ready(
328:     handles: &mut [(&dyn SelectHandle, usize, *const u8)],
329:     timeout: Timeout,
330:     is_biased: bool,
331: ) -> Option<usize> {
332:     if handles.is_empty() {
333:         // Wait until the timeout and return.
334:         match timeout {
335:             Timeout::Now => return None,
336:             Timeout::Never => {
337:                 utils::sleep_until(None);
338:                 unreachable!();
339:             }
340:             Timeout::At(when) => {
341:                 utils::sleep_until(Some(when));
342:                 return None;
343:             }
344:         }
345:     }
346: 
347:     if !is_biased {
348:         // Shuffle the operations for fairness.
349:         utils::shuffle(handles);
350:     }
351: 
352:     loop {
353:         let backoff = Backoff::new();
354:         loop {
355:             // Check operations for readiness.
356:             for &(handle, i, _) in handles.iter() {
357:                 if handle.is_ready() {
358:                     return Some(i);
359:                 }
360:             }
361: 
362:             if backoff.is_completed() {
363:                 break;
364:             } else {
365:                 backoff.snooze();
366:             }
367:         }
368: 
369:         // Check for timeout.
370:         match timeout {
371:             Timeout::Now => return None,
372:             Timeout::Never => {}
373:             Timeout::At(when) => {
374:                 if Instant::now() >= when {
375:                     return None;
376:                 }
377:             }
378:         }
379: 
380:         // Prepare for blocking.
381:         let res = Context::with(|cx| {
382:             let mut sel = Selected::Waiting;
383:             let mut registered_count = 0;
384: 
385:             // Begin watching all operations.
386:             for (handle, _, _) in handles.iter_mut() {
387:                 registered_count += 1;
388:                 let oper = Operation::hook::<&dyn SelectHandle>(handle);
389: 
390:                 // If registration returns `false`, that means the operation has just become ready.
391:                 if handle.watch(oper, cx) {
392:                     sel = match cx.try_select(Selected::Operation(oper)) {
393:                         Ok(()) => Selected::Operation(oper),
394:                         Err(s) => s,
395:                     };
396:                     break;
397:                 }
398: 
399:                 // If another thread has already chosen one of the operations, stop registration.
400:                 sel = cx.selected();
401:                 if sel != Selected::Waiting {
402:                     break;
403:                 }
404:             }
405: 
406:             if sel == Selected::Waiting {
407:                 // Check with each operation for how long we're allowed to block, and compute the
408:                 // earliest deadline.
409:                 let mut deadline: Option<Instant> = match timeout {
410:                     Timeout::Now => unreachable!(),
411:                     Timeout::Never => None,
412:                     Timeout::At(when) => Some(when),
413:                 };
414:                 for &(handle, _, _) in handles.iter() {
415:                     if let Some(x) = handle.deadline() {
416:                         deadline = deadline.map(|y| x.min(y)).or(Some(x));
417:                     }
418:                 }
419: 
420:                 // Block the current thread.
421:                 sel = cx.wait_until(deadline);
422:             }
423: 
424:             // Unwatch all operations.
425:             for (handle, _, _) in handles.iter_mut().take(registered_count) {
426:                 handle.unwatch(Operation::hook::<&dyn SelectHandle>(handle));
427:             }
428: 
429:             match sel {
430:                 Selected::Waiting => unreachable!(),
431:                 Selected::Aborted => {}
432:                 Selected::Disconnected => {}
433:                 Selected::Operation(_) => {
434:                     for (handle, i, _) in handles.iter_mut() {
435:                         let oper = Operation::hook::<&dyn SelectHandle>(handle);
436:                         if sel == Selected::Operation(oper) {
437:                             return Some(*i);
438:                         }
439:                     }
440:                 }
441:             }
442: 
443:             None
444:         });
445: 
446:         // Return if an operation became ready.
447:         if res.is_some() {
448:             return res;
449:         }
450:     }
451: }
452: 
453: /// Attempts to select one of the operations without blocking.
454: // This is a private API (exposed inside crossbeam_channel::internal module) that is used by the select macro.
455: #[inline]
456: pub fn try_select<'a>(
457:     handles: &mut [(&'a dyn SelectHandle, usize, *const u8)],
458:     is_biased: bool,
459: ) -> Result<SelectedOperation<'a>, TrySelectError> {
460:     match run_select(handles, Timeout::Now, is_biased) {
461:         None => Err(TrySelectError),
462:         Some((token, index, ptr)) => Ok(SelectedOperation {
463:             token,
464:             index,
465:             ptr,
466:             _marker: PhantomData,
467:         }),
468:     }
469: }
470: 
471: /// Blocks until one of the operations becomes ready and selects it.
472: // This is a private API (exposed inside crossbeam_channel::internal module) that is used by the select macro.
473: #[inline]
474: pub fn select<'a>(
475:     handles: &mut [(&'a dyn SelectHandle, usize, *const u8)],
476:     is_biased: bool,
477: ) -> SelectedOperation<'a> {
478:     if handles.is_empty() {
479:         panic!("no operations have been added to `Select`");
480:     }
481: 
482:     let (token, index, ptr) = run_select(handles, Timeout::Never, is_biased).unwrap();
483:     SelectedOperation {
484:         token,
485:         index,
486:         ptr,
487:         _marker: PhantomData,
488:     }
489: }
490: 
491: /// Blocks for a limited time until one of the operations becomes ready and selects it.
492: // This is a private API (exposed inside crossbeam_channel::internal module) that is used by the select macro.
493: #[inline]
494: pub fn select_timeout<'a>(
495:     handles: &mut [(&'a dyn SelectHandle, usize, *const u8)],
496:     timeout: Duration,
497:     is_biased: bool,
498: ) -> Result<SelectedOperation<'a>, SelectTimeoutError> {
499:     match Instant::now().checked_add(timeout) {
500:         Some(deadline) => select_deadline(handles, deadline, is_biased),
501:         None => Ok(select(handles, is_biased)),
502:     }
503: }
504: 
505: /// Blocks until a given deadline, or until one of the operations becomes ready and selects it.
506: #[inline]
507: pub(crate) fn select_deadline<'a>(
508:     handles: &mut [(&'a dyn SelectHandle, usize, *const u8)],
509:     deadline: Instant,
510:     is_biased: bool,
511: ) -> Result<SelectedOperation<'a>, SelectTimeoutError> {
512:     match run_select(handles, Timeout::At(deadline), is_biased) {
513:         None => Err(SelectTimeoutError),
514:         Some((token, index, ptr)) => Ok(SelectedOperation {
515:             token,
516:             index,
517:             ptr,
518:             _marker: PhantomData,
519:         }),
520:     }
521: }
522: 
523: /// Selects from a set of channel operations.
524: ///
525: /// `Select` allows you to define a set of channel operations, wait until any one of them becomes
526: /// ready, and finally execute it. If multiple operations are ready at the same time, a random one
527: /// among them is selected.
528: ///
529: /// An operation is considered to be ready if it doesn't have to block. Note that it is ready even
530: /// when it will simply return an error because the channel is disconnected.
531: ///
532: /// The [`select!`] macro is a convenience wrapper around `Select`. However, it cannot select over a
533: /// dynamically created list of channel operations.
534: ///
535: /// [`select!`]: crate::select!
536: ///
537: /// Once a list of operations has been built with `Select`, there are two different ways of
538: /// proceeding:
539: ///
540: /// * Select an operation with [`try_select`], [`select`], or [`select_timeout`]. If successful,
541: ///   the returned selected operation has already begun and **must** be completed. If we don't
542: ///   complete it, a panic will occur.
543: ///
544: /// * Wait for an operation to become ready with [`try_ready`], [`ready`], or [`ready_timeout`]. If
545: ///   successful, we may attempt to execute the operation, but are not obliged to. In fact, it's
546: ///   possible for another thread to make the operation not ready just before we try executing it,
547: ///   so it's wise to use a retry loop. However, note that these methods might return with success
548: ///   spuriously, so it's a good idea to always double check if the operation is really ready.
549: ///
550: /// # Examples
551: ///
552: /// Use [`select`] to receive a message from a list of receivers:
553: ///
554: /// ```
555: /// use crossbeam_channel::{Receiver, RecvError, Select};
556: ///
557: /// fn recv_multiple<T>(rs: &[Receiver<T>]) -> Result<T, RecvError> {
558: ///     // Build a list of operations.
559: ///     let mut sel = Select::new();
560: ///     for r in rs {
561: ///         sel.recv(r);
562: ///     }
563: ///
564: ///     // Complete the selected operation.
565: ///     let oper = sel.select();
566: ///     let index = oper.index();
567: ///     oper.recv(&rs[index])
568: /// }
569: /// ```
570: ///
571: /// Use [`ready`] to receive a message from a list of receivers:
572: ///
573: /// ```
574: /// use crossbeam_channel::{Receiver, RecvError, Select};
575: ///
576: /// fn recv_multiple<T>(rs: &[Receiver<T>]) -> Result<T, RecvError> {
577: ///     // Build a list of operations.
578: ///     let mut sel = Select::new();
579: ///     for r in rs {
580: ///         sel.recv(r);
581: ///     }
582: ///
583: ///     loop {
584: ///         // Wait until a receive operation becomes ready and try executing it.
585: ///         let index = sel.ready();
586: ///         let res = rs[index].try_recv();
587: ///
588: ///         // If the operation turns out not to be ready, retry.
589: ///         if let Err(e) = res {
590: ///             if e.is_empty() {
591: ///                 continue;
592: ///             }
593: ///         }
594: ///
595: ///         // Success!
596: ///         return res.map_err(|_| RecvError);
597: ///     }
598: /// }
599: /// ```
600: ///
601: /// [`try_select`]: Select::try_select
602: /// [`select`]: Select::select
603: /// [`select_timeout`]: Select::select_timeout
604: /// [`try_ready`]: Select::try_ready
605: /// [`ready`]: Select::ready
606: /// [`ready_timeout`]: Select::ready_timeout
607: pub struct Select<'a> {
608:     /// A list of senders and receivers participating in selection.
609:     handles: Vec<(&'a dyn SelectHandle, usize, *const u8)>,
610: 
611:     /// The next index to assign to an operation.
612:     next_index: usize,
613: 
614:     /// Whether to use the index of handles as bias for selecting ready operations.
615:     biased: bool,
616: }
617: 
618: unsafe impl Send for Select<'_> {}
619: unsafe impl Sync for Select<'_> {}
620: 
621: impl<'a> Select<'a> {
622:     /// Creates an empty list of channel operations for selection.
623:     ///
624:     /// # Examples
625:     ///
626:     /// ```
627:     /// use crossbeam_channel::Select;
628:     ///
629:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
630:     ///
631:     /// // The list of operations is empty, which means no operation can be selected.
632:     /// assert!(sel.try_select().is_err());
633:     /// ```
634:     pub fn new() -> Self {
635:         Self {
636:             handles: Vec::with_capacity(4),
637:             next_index: 0,
638:             biased: false,
639:         }
640:     }
641: 
642:     /// Creates an empty list of channel operations with biased selection.
643:     ///
644:     /// When multiple handles are ready, this will select the operation with the lowest index.
645:     ///
646:     /// # Examples
647:     ///
648:     /// ```
649:     /// use crossbeam_channel::Select;
650:     ///
651:     /// let mut sel = Select::new_biased();
652:     ///
653:     /// // The list of operations is empty, which means no operation can be selected.
654:     /// assert!(sel.try_select().is_err());
655:     /// ```
656:     pub fn new_biased() -> Self {
657:         Self {
658:             biased: true,
659:             ..Default::default()
660:         }
661:     }
662: 
663:     /// Adds a send operation.
664:     ///
665:     /// Returns the index of the added operation.
666:     ///
667:     /// # Examples
668:     ///
669:     /// ```
670:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
671:     ///
672:     /// let (s, r) = unbounded::<i32>();
673:     ///
674:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
675:     /// let index = sel.send(&s);
676:     /// ```
677:     pub fn send<T>(&mut self, s: &'a Sender<T>) -> usize {
678:         let i = self.next_index;
679:         let ptr = s as *const Sender<_> as *const u8;
680:         self.handles.push((s, i, ptr));
681:         self.next_index += 1;
682:         i
683:     }
684: 
685:     /// Adds a receive operation.
686:     ///
687:     /// Returns the index of the added operation.
688:     ///
689:     /// # Examples
690:     ///
691:     /// ```
692:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
693:     ///
694:     /// let (s, r) = unbounded::<i32>();
695:     ///
696:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
697:     /// let index = sel.recv(&r);
698:     /// ```
699:     pub fn recv<T>(&mut self, r: &'a Receiver<T>) -> usize {
700:         let i = self.next_index;
701:         let ptr = r as *const Receiver<_> as *const u8;
702:         self.handles.push((r, i, ptr));
703:         self.next_index += 1;
704:         i
705:     }
706: 
707:     /// Removes a previously added operation.
708:     ///
709:     /// This is useful when an operation is selected because the channel got disconnected and we
710:     /// want to try again to select a different operation instead.
711:     ///
712:     /// If new operations are added after removing some, the indices of removed operations will not
713:     /// be reused.
714:     ///
715:     /// # Panics
716:     ///
717:     /// An attempt to remove a non-existing or already removed operation will panic.
718:     ///
719:     /// # Examples
720:     ///
721:     /// ```
722:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
723:     ///
724:     /// let (s1, r1) = unbounded::<i32>();
725:     /// let (_, r2) = unbounded::<i32>();
726:     ///
727:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
728:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r1);
729:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
730:     ///
731:     /// // Both operations are initially ready, so a random one will be executed.
732:     /// let oper = sel.select();
733:     /// assert_eq!(oper.index(), oper2);
734:     /// assert!(oper.recv(&r2).is_err());
735:     /// sel.remove(oper2);
736:     ///
737:     /// s1.send(10).unwrap();
738:     ///
739:     /// let oper = sel.select();
740:     /// assert_eq!(oper.index(), oper1);
741:     /// assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r1), Ok(10));
742:     /// ```
743:     pub fn remove(&mut self, index: usize) {
744:         assert!(
745:             index < self.next_index,
746:             "index out of bounds; {} >= {}",
747:             index,
748:             self.next_index,
749:         );
750: 
751:         let i = self
752:             .handles
753:             .iter()
754:             .enumerate()
755:             .find(|(_, (_, i, _))| *i == index)
756:             .expect("no operation with this index")
757:             .0;
758: 
759:         self.handles.swap_remove(i);
760:     }
761: 
762:     /// Attempts to select one of the operations without blocking.
763:     ///
764:     /// If an operation is ready, it is selected and returned. If multiple operations are ready at
765:     /// the same time, a random one among them is selected. If none of the operations are ready, an
766:     /// error is returned.
767:     ///
768:     /// An operation is considered to be ready if it doesn't have to block. Note that it is ready
769:     /// even when it will simply return an error because the channel is disconnected.
770:     ///
771:     /// The selected operation must be completed with [`SelectedOperation::send`]
772:     /// or [`SelectedOperation::recv`].
773:     ///
774:     /// # Examples
775:     ///
776:     /// ```
777:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
778:     ///
779:     /// let (s1, r1) = unbounded();
780:     /// let (s2, r2) = unbounded();
781:     ///
782:     /// s1.send(10).unwrap();
783:     /// s2.send(20).unwrap();
784:     ///
785:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
786:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r1);
787:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
788:     ///
789:     /// // Both operations are initially ready, so a random one will be executed.
790:     /// let oper = sel.try_select();
791:     /// match oper {
792:     ///     Err(_) => panic!("both operations should be ready"),
793:     ///     Ok(oper) => match oper.index() {
794:     ///         i if i == oper1 => assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r1), Ok(10)),
795:     ///         i if i == oper2 => assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r2), Ok(20)),
796:     ///         _ => unreachable!(),
797:     ///     }
798:     /// }
799:     /// ```
800:     pub fn try_select(&mut self) -> Result<SelectedOperation<'a>, TrySelectError> {
801:         try_select(&mut self.handles, self.biased)
802:     }

804:     /// Blocks until one of the operations becomes ready and selects it.
805:     ///
806:     /// Once an operation becomes ready, it is selected and returned. If multiple operations are
807:     /// ready at the same time, a random one among them is selected.
808:     ///
809:     /// An operation is considered to be ready if it doesn't have to block. Note that it is ready
810:     /// even when it will simply return an error because the channel is disconnected.
811:     ///
812:     /// The selected operation must be completed with [`SelectedOperation::send`]
813:     /// or [`SelectedOperation::recv`].
814:     ///
815:     /// # Panics
816:     ///
817:     /// Panics if no operations have been added to `Select`.
818:     ///
819:     /// # Examples
820:     ///
821:     /// ```
822:     /// use std::thread;
823:     /// use std::time::Duration;
824:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
825:     ///
826:     /// let (s1, r1) = unbounded();
827:     /// let (s2, r2) = unbounded();
828:     ///
829:     /// # let t1 =
830:     /// thread::spawn(move || {
831:     ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
832:     ///     s1.send(10).unwrap();
833:     /// });
834:     /// # let t2 =
835:     /// thread::spawn(move || s2.send(20).unwrap());
836:     ///
837:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
838:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r1);
839:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
840:     ///
841:     /// // The second operation will be selected because it becomes ready first.
842:     /// let oper = sel.select();
843:     /// match oper.index() {
844:     ///     i if i == oper1 => assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r1), Ok(10)),
845:     ///     i if i == oper2 => assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r2), Ok(20)),
846:     ///     _ => unreachable!(),
847:     /// }
848:     /// # t1.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
849:     /// # t2.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
850:     /// ```
851:     pub fn select(&mut self) -> SelectedOperation<'a> {
852:         select(&mut self.handles, self.biased)
853:     }

855:     /// Blocks for a limited time until one of the operations becomes ready and selects it.
856:     ///
857:     /// If an operation becomes ready, it is selected and returned. If multiple operations are
858:     /// ready at the same time, a random one among them is selected. If none of the operations
859:     /// become ready for the specified duration, an error is returned.
860:     ///
861:     /// An operation is considered to be ready if it doesn't have to block. Note that it is ready
862:     /// even when it will simply return an error because the channel is disconnected.
863:     ///
864:     /// The selected operation must be completed with [`SelectedOperation::send`]
865:     /// or [`SelectedOperation::recv`].
866:     ///
867:     /// # Examples
868:     ///
869:     /// ```
870:     /// use std::thread;
871:     /// use std::time::Duration;
872:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
873:     ///
874:     /// let (s1, r1) = unbounded();
875:     /// let (s2, r2) = unbounded();
876:     ///
877:     /// # let t1 =
878:     /// thread::spawn(move || {
879:     ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
880:     ///     s1.send(10).unwrap();
881:     /// });
882:     /// # let t2 =
883:     /// thread::spawn(move || s2.send(20).unwrap());
884:     ///
885:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
886:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r1);
887:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
888:     ///
889:     /// // The second operation will be selected because it becomes ready first.
890:     /// let oper = sel.select_timeout(Duration::from_millis(500));
891:     /// match oper {
892:     ///     Err(_) => panic!("should not have timed out"),
893:     ///     Ok(oper) => match oper.index() {
894:     ///         i if i == oper1 => assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r1), Ok(10)),
895:     ///         i if i == oper2 => assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r2), Ok(20)),
896:     ///         _ => unreachable!(),
897:     ///     }
898:     /// }
899:     /// # t1.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
900:     /// # t2.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
901:     /// ```
902:     pub fn select_timeout(
903:         &mut self,
904:         timeout: Duration,
905:     ) -> Result<SelectedOperation<'a>, SelectTimeoutError> {
906:         select_timeout(&mut self.handles, timeout, self.biased)
907:     }

909:     /// Blocks until a given deadline, or until one of the operations becomes ready and selects it.
910:     ///
911:     /// If an operation becomes ready, it is selected and returned. If multiple operations are
912:     /// ready at the same time, a random one among them is selected. If none of the operations
913:     /// become ready before the given deadline, an error is returned.
914:     ///
915:     /// An operation is considered to be ready if it doesn't have to block. Note that it is ready
916:     /// even when it will simply return an error because the channel is disconnected.
917:     ///
918:     /// The selected operation must be completed with [`SelectedOperation::send`]
919:     /// or [`SelectedOperation::recv`].
920:     ///
921:     /// # Examples
922:     ///
923:     /// ```
924:     /// use std::thread;
925:     /// use std::time::{Instant, Duration};
926:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
927:     ///
928:     /// let (s1, r1) = unbounded();
929:     /// let (s2, r2) = unbounded();
930:     ///
931:     /// # let t1 =
932:     /// thread::spawn(move || {
933:     ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
934:     ///     s1.send(10).unwrap();
935:     /// });
936:     /// # let t2 =
937:     /// thread::spawn(move || s2.send(20).unwrap());
938:     ///
939:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
940:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r1);
941:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
942:     ///
943:     /// let deadline = Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(500);
944:     ///
945:     /// // The second operation will be selected because it becomes ready first.
946:     /// let oper = sel.select_deadline(deadline);
947:     /// match oper {
948:     ///     Err(_) => panic!("should not have timed out"),
949:     ///     Ok(oper) => match oper.index() {
950:     ///         i if i == oper1 => assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r1), Ok(10)),
951:     ///         i if i == oper2 => assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r2), Ok(20)),
952:     ///         _ => unreachable!(),
953:     ///     }
954:     /// }
955:     /// # t1.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
956:     /// # t2.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
957:     /// ```
958:     pub fn select_deadline(
959:         &mut self,
960:         deadline: Instant,
961:     ) -> Result<SelectedOperation<'a>, SelectTimeoutError> {
962:         select_deadline(&mut self.handles, deadline, self.biased)
963:     }

965:     /// Attempts to find a ready operation without blocking.
966:     ///
967:     /// If an operation is ready, its index is returned. If multiple operations are ready at the
968:     /// same time, a random one among them is chosen. If none of the operations are ready, an error
969:     /// is returned.
970:     ///
971:     /// An operation is considered to be ready if it doesn't have to block. Note that it is ready
972:     /// even when it will simply return an error because the channel is disconnected.
973:     ///
974:     /// Note that this method might return with success spuriously, so it's a good idea to always
975:     /// double check if the operation is really ready.
976:     ///
977:     /// # Examples
978:     ///
979:     /// ```
980:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
981:     ///
982:     /// let (s1, r1) = unbounded();
983:     /// let (s2, r2) = unbounded();
984:     ///
985:     /// s1.send(10).unwrap();
986:     /// s2.send(20).unwrap();
987:     ///
988:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
989:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r1);
990:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
991:     ///
992:     /// // Both operations are initially ready, so a random one will be chosen.
993:     /// match sel.try_ready() {
994:     ///     Err(_) => panic!("both operations should be ready"),
995:     ///     Ok(i) if i == oper1 => assert_eq!(r1.try_recv(), Ok(10)),
996:     ///     Ok(i) if i == oper2 => assert_eq!(r2.try_recv(), Ok(20)),
997:     ///     Ok(_) => unreachable!(),
998:     /// }
999:     /// ```
1000:     pub fn try_ready(&mut self) -> Result<usize, TryReadyError> {
1001:         match run_ready(&mut self.handles, Timeout::Now, self.biased) {
1002:             None => Err(TryReadyError),
1003:             Some(index) => Ok(index),
1004:         }
1005:     }

1007:     /// Blocks until one of the operations becomes ready.
1008:     ///
1009:     /// Once an operation becomes ready, its index is returned. If multiple operations are ready at
1010:     /// the same time, a random one among them is chosen.
1011:     ///
1012:     /// An operation is considered to be ready if it doesn't have to block. Note that it is ready
1013:     /// even when it will simply return an error because the channel is disconnected.
1014:     ///
1015:     /// Note that this method might return with success spuriously, so it's a good idea to always
1016:     /// double check if the operation is really ready.
1017:     ///
1018:     /// # Panics
1019:     ///
1020:     /// Panics if no operations have been added to `Select`.
1021:     ///
1022:     /// # Examples
1023:     ///
1024:     /// ```
1025:     /// use std::thread;
1026:     /// use std::time::Duration;
1027:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
1028:     ///
1029:     /// let (s1, r1) = unbounded();
1030:     /// let (s2, r2) = unbounded();
1031:     ///
1032:     /// # let t1 =
1033:     /// thread::spawn(move || {
1034:     ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
1035:     ///     s1.send(10).unwrap();
1036:     /// });
1037:     /// # let t2 =
1038:     /// thread::spawn(move || s2.send(20).unwrap());
1039:     ///
1040:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
1041:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r1);
1042:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
1043:     ///
1044:     /// // The second operation will be selected because it becomes ready first.
1045:     /// match sel.ready() {
1046:     ///     i if i == oper1 => assert_eq!(r1.try_recv(), Ok(10)),
1047:     ///     i if i == oper2 => assert_eq!(r2.try_recv(), Ok(20)),
1048:     ///     _ => unreachable!(),
1049:     /// }
1050:     /// # t1.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
1051:     /// # t2.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
1052:     /// ```
1053:     pub fn ready(&mut self) -> usize {
1054:         if self.handles.is_empty() {
1055:             panic!("no operations have been added to `Select`");
1056:         }
1057: 
1058:         run_ready(&mut self.handles, Timeout::Never, self.biased).unwrap()
1059:     }

1061:     /// Blocks for a limited time until one of the operations becomes ready.
1062:     ///
1063:     /// If an operation becomes ready, its index is returned. If multiple operations are ready at
1064:     /// the same time, a random one among them is chosen. If none of the operations become ready
1065:     /// for the specified duration, an error is returned.
1066:     ///
1067:     /// An operation is considered to be ready if it doesn't have to block. Note that it is ready
1068:     /// even when it will simply return an error because the channel is disconnected.
1069:     ///
1070:     /// Note that this method might return with success spuriously, so it's a good idea to double
1071:     /// check if the operation is really ready.
1072:     ///
1073:     /// # Examples
1074:     ///
1075:     /// ```
1076:     /// use std::thread;
1077:     /// use std::time::Duration;
1078:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
1079:     ///
1080:     /// let (s1, r1) = unbounded();
1081:     /// let (s2, r2) = unbounded();
1082:     ///
1083:     /// # let t1 =
1084:     /// thread::spawn(move || {
1085:     ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
1086:     ///     s1.send(10).unwrap();
1087:     /// });
1088:     /// # let t2 =
1089:     /// thread::spawn(move || s2.send(20).unwrap());
1090:     ///
1091:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
1092:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r1);
1093:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
1094:     ///
1095:     /// // The second operation will be selected because it becomes ready first.
1096:     /// match sel.ready_timeout(Duration::from_millis(500)) {
1097:     ///     Err(_) => panic!("should not have timed out"),
1098:     ///     Ok(i) if i == oper1 => assert_eq!(r1.try_recv(), Ok(10)),
1099:     ///     Ok(i) if i == oper2 => assert_eq!(r2.try_recv(), Ok(20)),
1100:     ///     Ok(_) => unreachable!(),
1101:     /// }
1102:     /// # t1.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
1103:     /// # t2.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
1104:     /// ```
1105:     pub fn ready_timeout(&mut self, timeout: Duration) -> Result<usize, ReadyTimeoutError> {
1106:         match Instant::now().checked_add(timeout) {
1107:             Some(deadline) => self.ready_deadline(deadline),
1108:             None => Ok(self.ready()),
1109:         }
1110:     }

1112:     /// Blocks until a given deadline, or until one of the operations becomes ready.
1113:     ///
1114:     /// If an operation becomes ready, its index is returned. If multiple operations are ready at
1115:     /// the same time, a random one among them is chosen. If none of the operations become ready
1116:     /// before the deadline, an error is returned.
1117:     ///
1118:     /// An operation is considered to be ready if it doesn't have to block. Note that it is ready
1119:     /// even when it will simply return an error because the channel is disconnected.
1120:     ///
1121:     /// Note that this method might return with success spuriously, so it's a good idea to double
1122:     /// check if the operation is really ready.
1123:     ///
1124:     /// # Examples
1125:     ///
1126:     /// ```
1127:     /// use std::thread;
1128:     /// use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
1129:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{unbounded, Select};
1130:     ///
1131:     /// let deadline = Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(500);
1132:     ///
1133:     /// let (s1, r1) = unbounded();
1134:     /// let (s2, r2) = unbounded();
1135:     ///
1136:     /// # let t1 =
1137:     /// thread::spawn(move || {
1138:     ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
1139:     ///     s1.send(10).unwrap();
1140:     /// });
1141:     /// # let t2 =
1142:     /// thread::spawn(move || s2.send(20).unwrap());
1143:     ///
1144:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
1145:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r1);
1146:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
1147:     ///
1148:     /// // The second operation will be selected because it becomes ready first.
1149:     /// match sel.ready_deadline(deadline) {
1150:     ///     Err(_) => panic!("should not have timed out"),
1151:     ///     Ok(i) if i == oper1 => assert_eq!(r1.try_recv(), Ok(10)),
1152:     ///     Ok(i) if i == oper2 => assert_eq!(r2.try_recv(), Ok(20)),
1153:     ///     Ok(_) => unreachable!(),
1154:     /// }
1155:     /// # t1.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
1156:     /// # t2.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
1157:     /// ```
1158:     pub fn ready_deadline(&mut self, deadline: Instant) -> Result<usize, ReadyTimeoutError> {
1159:         match run_ready(&mut self.handles, Timeout::At(deadline), self.biased) {
1160:             None => Err(ReadyTimeoutError),
1161:             Some(index) => Ok(index),
1162:         }
1163:     }
1164: }

1167:     fn clone(&self) -> Self {

1169:             handles: self.handles.clone(),

1171:             biased: self.biased,
1172:         }
1173:     }
1174: }

1176: impl Default for Select<'_> {

1188: /// A selected operation that needs to be completed.
1189: ///
1190: /// To complete the operation, call [`send`] or [`recv`].
1191: ///
1192: /// # Panics
1193: ///
1194: /// Forgetting to complete the operation is an error and might lead to deadlocks. If a
1195: /// `SelectedOperation` is dropped without completion, a panic occurs.
1196: ///
1197: /// [`send`]: SelectedOperation::send
1198: /// [`recv`]: SelectedOperation::recv
1199: #[must_use]
1200: pub struct SelectedOperation<'a> {
1201:     /// Token needed to complete the operation.
1202:     token: Token,
1203: 
1204:     /// The index of the selected operation.
1205:     index: usize,
1206: 
1207:     /// The address of the selected `Sender` or `Receiver`.
1208:     ptr: *const u8,
1209: 
1210:     /// Indicates that `Sender`s and `Receiver`s are borrowed.
1211:     _marker: PhantomData<&'a ()>,
1212: }
1213: 
1214: impl SelectedOperation<'_> {
1215:     /// Returns the index of the selected operation.
1216:     ///
1217:     /// # Examples
1218:     ///
1219:     /// ```
1220:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, Select};
1221:     ///
1222:     /// let (s1, r1) = bounded::<()>(0);
1223:     /// let (s2, r2) = bounded::<()>(0);
1224:     /// let (s3, r3) = bounded::<()>(1);
1225:     ///
1226:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
1227:     /// let oper1 = sel.send(&s1);
1228:     /// let oper2 = sel.recv(&r2);
1229:     /// let oper3 = sel.send(&s3);
1230:     ///
1231:     /// // Only the last operation is ready.
1232:     /// let oper = sel.select();
1233:     /// assert_eq!(oper.index(), 2);
1234:     /// assert_eq!(oper.index(), oper3);
1235:     ///
1236:     /// // Complete the operation.
1237:     /// oper.send(&s3, ()).unwrap();
1238:     /// ```
1239:     pub fn index(&self) -> usize {
1240:         self.index
1241:     }
1242: 
1243:     /// Completes the send operation.
1244:     ///
1245:     /// The passed [`Sender`] reference must be the same one that was used in [`Select::send`]
1246:     /// when the operation was added.
1247:     ///
1248:     /// # Panics
1249:     ///
1250:     /// Panics if an incorrect [`Sender`] reference is passed.
1251:     ///
1252:     /// # Examples
1253:     ///
1254:     /// ```
1255:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, Select, SendError};
1256:     ///
1257:     /// let (s, r) = bounded::<i32>(0);
1258:     /// drop(r);
1259:     ///
1260:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
1261:     /// let oper1 = sel.send(&s);
1262:     ///
1263:     /// let oper = sel.select();
1264:     /// assert_eq!(oper.index(), oper1);
1265:     /// assert_eq!(oper.send(&s, 10), Err(SendError(10)));
1266:     /// ```
1267:     pub fn send<T>(mut self, s: &Sender<T>, msg: T) -> Result<(), SendError<T>> {
1268:         assert!(
1269:             s as *const Sender<T> as *const u8 == self.ptr,
1270:             "passed a sender that wasn't selected",
1271:         );
1272:         let res = unsafe { channel::write(s, &mut self.token, msg) };
1273:         mem::forget(self);
1274:         res.map_err(SendError)
1275:     }
1276: 
1277:     /// Completes the receive operation.
1278:     ///
1279:     /// The passed [`Receiver`] reference must be the same one that was used in [`Select::recv`]
1280:     /// when the operation was added.
1281:     ///
1282:     /// # Panics
1283:     ///
1284:     /// Panics if an incorrect [`Receiver`] reference is passed.
1285:     ///
1286:     /// # Examples
1287:     ///
1288:     /// ```
1289:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, Select, RecvError};
1290:     ///
1291:     /// let (s, r) = bounded::<i32>(0);
1292:     /// drop(s);
1293:     ///
1294:     /// let mut sel = Select::new();
1295:     /// let oper1 = sel.recv(&r);
1296:     ///
1297:     /// let oper = sel.select();
1298:     /// assert_eq!(oper.index(), oper1);
1299:     /// assert_eq!(oper.recv(&r), Err(RecvError));
1300:     /// ```
1301:     pub fn recv<T>(mut self, r: &Receiver<T>) -> Result<T, RecvError> {
1302:         assert!(
1303:             r as *const Receiver<T> as *const u8 == self.ptr,
1304:             "passed a receiver that wasn't selected",
1305:         );
1306:         let res = unsafe { channel::read(r, &mut self.token) };
1307:         mem::forget(self);
1308:         res.map_err(|_| RecvError)
1309:     }
1310: }

21: /// Creates a multi-producer multi-consumer channel of unbounded capacity.
22: ///
23: /// This channel has a growable buffer that can hold any number of messages at a time.
24: ///
25: /// For more info on how to use the channel see [module level documentation](index.html).
26: ///
27: /// # Examples
28: ///
29: /// ```
30: /// use std::thread;
31: /// use crossbeam_channel::unbounded;
32: ///
33: /// let (s, r) = unbounded();
34: ///
35: /// // Computes the n-th Fibonacci number.
36: /// fn fib(n: i32) -> i32 {
37: ///     if n <= 1 {
38: ///         n
39: ///     } else {
40: ///         fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2)
41: ///     }
42: /// }
43: ///
44: /// // Spawn an asynchronous computation.
45: /// thread::spawn(move || s.send(fib(20)).unwrap());
46: ///
47: /// // Print the result of the computation.
48: /// println!("{}", r.recv().unwrap());
49: /// ```
50: pub fn unbounded<T>() -> (Sender<T>, Receiver<T>) {
51:     let (s, r) = counter::new(flavors::list::Channel::new());
52:     let s = Sender {
53:         flavor: SenderFlavor::List(s),
54:     };
55:     let r = Receiver {
56:         flavor: ReceiverFlavor::List(r),
57:     };
58:     (s, r)
59: }
60: 
61: /// Creates a multi-producer multi-consumer channel of bounded capacity.
62: ///
63: /// This channel has a buffer that can hold at most `cap` messages at a time.
64: ///
65: /// A special case is zero-capacity channel, which cannot hold any messages. Instead, send and
66: /// receive operations must appear at the same time in order to pair up and pass the message over.
67: ///
68: /// For more info on how to use the channel see [module level documentation](index.html).
69: ///
70: /// # Examples
71: ///
72: /// A channel of capacity 1:
73: ///
74: /// ```
75: /// use std::thread;
76: /// use std::time::Duration;
77: /// use crossbeam_channel::bounded;
78: ///
79: /// let (s, r) = bounded(1);
80: ///
81: /// // This call returns immediately because there is enough space in the channel.
82: /// s.send(1).unwrap();
83: ///
84: /// thread::spawn(move || {
85: ///     // This call blocks the current thread because the channel is full.
86: ///     // It will be able to complete only after the first message is received.
87: ///     s.send(2).unwrap();
88: /// });
89: ///
90: /// thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
91: /// assert_eq!(r.recv(), Ok(1));
92: /// assert_eq!(r.recv(), Ok(2));
93: /// ```
94: ///
95: /// A zero-capacity channel:
96: ///
97: /// ```
98: /// use std::thread;
99: /// use std::time::Duration;
100: /// use crossbeam_channel::bounded;
101: ///
102: /// let (s, r) = bounded(0);
103: ///
104: /// thread::spawn(move || {
105: ///     // This call blocks the current thread until a receive operation appears
106: ///     // on the other side of the channel.
107: ///     s.send(1).unwrap();
108: /// });
109: ///
110: /// thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
111: /// assert_eq!(r.recv(), Ok(1));
112: /// ```
113: pub fn bounded<T>(cap: usize) -> (Sender<T>, Receiver<T>) {
114:     if cap == 0 {
115:         let (s, r) = counter::new(flavors::zero::Channel::new());
116:         let s = Sender {
117:             flavor: SenderFlavor::Zero(s),
118:         };
119:         let r = Receiver {
120:             flavor: ReceiverFlavor::Zero(r),
121:         };
122:         (s, r)
123:     } else {
124:         let (s, r) = counter::new(flavors::array::Channel::with_capacity(cap));
125:         let s = Sender {
126:             flavor: SenderFlavor::Array(s),
127:         };
128:         let r = Receiver {
129:             flavor: ReceiverFlavor::Array(r),
130:         };
131:         (s, r)
132:     }
133: }
134: 
135: /// Creates a receiver that delivers a message after a certain duration of time.
136: ///
137: /// The channel is bounded with capacity of 1 and never gets disconnected. Exactly one message will
138: /// be sent into the channel after `duration` elapses. The message is the instant at which it is
139: /// sent.
140: ///
141: /// # Examples
142: ///
143: /// Using an `after` channel for timeouts:
144: ///
145: /// ```
146: /// use std::time::Duration;
147: /// use crossbeam_channel::{after, select, unbounded};
148: ///
149: /// let (s, r) = unbounded::<i32>();
150: /// let timeout = Duration::from_millis(100);
151: ///
152: /// select! {
153: ///     recv(r) -> msg => println!("received {:?}", msg),
154: ///     recv(after(timeout)) -> _ => println!("timed out"),
155: /// }
156: /// ```
157: ///
158: /// When the message gets sent:
159: ///
160: /// ```
161: /// use std::thread;
162: /// use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
163: /// use crossbeam_channel::after;
164: ///
165: /// // Converts a number of milliseconds into a `Duration`.
166: /// let ms = |ms| Duration::from_millis(ms);
167: ///
168: /// // Returns `true` if `a` and `b` are very close `Instant`s.
169: /// let eq = |a, b| a + ms(60) > b && b + ms(60) > a;
170: ///
171: /// let start = Instant::now();
172: /// let r = after(ms(100));
173: ///
174: /// thread::sleep(ms(500));
175: ///
176: /// // This message was sent 100 ms from the start and received 500 ms from the start.
177: /// assert!(eq(r.recv().unwrap(), start + ms(100)));
178: /// assert!(eq(Instant::now(), start + ms(500)));
179: /// ```
180: pub fn after(duration: Duration) -> Receiver<Instant> {
181:     match Instant::now().checked_add(duration) {
182:         Some(deadline) => Receiver {
183:             flavor: ReceiverFlavor::At(Arc::new(flavors::at::Channel::new_deadline(deadline))),
184:         },
185:         None => never(),
186:     }
187: }
188: 
189: /// Creates a receiver that delivers a message at a certain instant in time.
190: ///
191: /// The channel is bounded with capacity of 1 and never gets disconnected. Exactly one message will
192: /// be sent into the channel at the moment in time `when`. The message is the instant at which it
193: /// is sent, which is the same as `when`. If `when` is in the past, the message will be delivered
194: /// instantly to the receiver.
195: ///
196: /// # Examples
197: ///
198: /// Using an `at` channel for timeouts:
199: ///
200: /// ```
201: /// use std::time::{Instant, Duration};
202: /// use crossbeam_channel::{at, select, unbounded};
203: ///
204: /// let (s, r) = unbounded::<i32>();
205: /// let deadline = Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(500);
206: ///
207: /// select! {
208: ///     recv(r) -> msg => println!("received {:?}", msg),
209: ///     recv(at(deadline)) -> _ => println!("timed out"),
210: /// }
211: /// ```
212: ///
213: /// When the message gets sent:
214: ///
215: /// ```
216: /// use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
217: /// use crossbeam_channel::at;
218: ///
219: /// // Converts a number of milliseconds into a `Duration`.
220: /// let ms = |ms| Duration::from_millis(ms);
221: ///
222: /// let start = Instant::now();
223: /// let end = start + ms(100);
224: ///
225: /// let r = at(end);
226: ///
227: /// // This message was sent 100 ms from the start
228: /// assert_eq!(r.recv().unwrap(), end);
229: /// assert!(Instant::now() > start + ms(100));
230: /// ```
231: pub fn at(when: Instant) -> Receiver<Instant> {
232:     Receiver {
233:         flavor: ReceiverFlavor::At(Arc::new(flavors::at::Channel::new_deadline(when))),
234:     }
235: }
236: 
237: /// Creates a receiver that never delivers messages.
238: ///
239: /// The channel is bounded with capacity of 0 and never gets disconnected.
240: ///
241: /// # Examples
242: ///
243: /// Using a `never` channel to optionally add a timeout to [`select!`]:
244: ///
245: /// [`select!`]: crate::select!
246: ///
247: /// ```
248: /// use std::thread;
249: /// use std::time::Duration;
250: /// use crossbeam_channel::{after, select, never, unbounded};
251: ///
252: /// let (s, r) = unbounded();
253: ///
254: /// # let t =
255: /// thread::spawn(move || {
256: ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
257: ///     s.send(1).unwrap();
258: /// });
259: ///
260: /// // Suppose this duration can be a `Some` or a `None`.
261: /// let duration = Some(Duration::from_millis(100));
262: ///
263: /// // Create a channel that times out after the specified duration.
264: /// let timeout = duration
265: ///     .map(after)
266: ///     .unwrap_or_else(never);
267: ///
268: /// select! {
269: ///     recv(r) -> msg => assert_eq!(msg, Ok(1)),
270: ///     recv(timeout) -> _ => println!("timed out"),
271: /// }
272: /// # t.join().unwrap(); // join thread to avoid https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/1371
273: /// ```
274: pub fn never<T>() -> Receiver<T> {
275:     Receiver {
276:         flavor: ReceiverFlavor::Never(flavors::never::Channel::new()),
277:     }
278: }
279: 
280: /// Creates a receiver that delivers messages periodically.
281: ///
282: /// The channel is bounded with capacity of 1 and never gets disconnected. Messages will be
283: /// sent into the channel in intervals of `duration`. Each message is the instant at which it is
284: /// sent.
285: ///
286: /// # Examples
287: ///
288: /// Using a `tick` channel to periodically print elapsed time:
289: ///
290: /// ```
291: /// use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
292: /// use crossbeam_channel::tick;
293: ///
294: /// let start = Instant::now();
295: /// let ticker = tick(Duration::from_millis(100));
296: ///
297: /// for _ in 0..5 {
298: ///     ticker.recv().unwrap();
299: ///     println!("elapsed: {:?}", start.elapsed());
300: /// }
301: /// ```
302: ///
303: /// When messages get sent:
304: ///
305: /// ```
306: /// use std::thread;
307: /// use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
308: /// use crossbeam_channel::tick;
309: ///
310: /// // Converts a number of milliseconds into a `Duration`.
311: /// let ms = |ms| Duration::from_millis(ms);
312: ///
313: /// // Returns `true` if `a` and `b` are very close `Instant`s.
314: /// let eq = |a, b| a + ms(65) > b && b + ms(65) > a;
315: ///
316: /// let start = Instant::now();
317: /// let r = tick(ms(100));
318: ///
319: /// // This message was sent 100 ms from the start and received 100 ms from the start.
320: /// assert!(eq(r.recv().unwrap(), start + ms(100)));
321: /// assert!(eq(Instant::now(), start + ms(100)));
322: ///
323: /// thread::sleep(ms(500));
324: ///
325: /// // This message was sent 200 ms from the start and received 600 ms from the start.
326: /// assert!(eq(r.recv().unwrap(), start + ms(200)));
327: /// assert!(eq(Instant::now(), start + ms(600)));
328: ///
329: /// // This message was sent 700 ms from the start and received 700 ms from the start.
330: /// assert!(eq(r.recv().unwrap(), start + ms(700)));
331: /// assert!(eq(Instant::now(), start + ms(700)));
332: /// ```
333: pub fn tick(duration: Duration) -> Receiver<Instant> {
334:     match Instant::now().checked_add(duration) {
335:         Some(delivery_time) => Receiver {
336:             flavor: ReceiverFlavor::Tick(Arc::new(flavors::tick::Channel::new(
337:                 delivery_time,
338:                 duration,
339:             ))),
340:         },
341:         None => never(),
342:     }
343: }
344: 
345: /// The sending side of a channel.
346: ///
347: /// # Examples
348: ///
349: /// ```
350: /// use std::thread;
351: /// use crossbeam_channel::unbounded;
352: ///
353: /// let (s1, r) = unbounded();
354: /// let s2 = s1.clone();
355: ///
356: /// thread::spawn(move || s1.send(1).unwrap());
357: /// thread::spawn(move || s2.send(2).unwrap());
358: ///
359: /// let msg1 = r.recv().unwrap();
360: /// let msg2 = r.recv().unwrap();
361: ///
362: /// assert_eq!(msg1 + msg2, 3);
363: /// ```
364: pub struct Sender<T> {
365:     flavor: SenderFlavor<T>,
366: }
367: 
368: /// Sender flavors.
369: enum SenderFlavor<T> {
370:     /// Bounded channel based on a preallocated array.
371:     Array(counter::Sender<flavors::array::Channel<T>>),
372: 
373:     /// Unbounded channel implemented as a linked list.
374:     List(counter::Sender<flavors::list::Channel<T>>),
375: 
376:     /// Zero-capacity channel.
377:     Zero(counter::Sender<flavors::zero::Channel<T>>),
378: }
379: 
380: unsafe impl<T: Send> Send for Sender<T> {}
381: unsafe impl<T: Send> Sync for Sender<T> {}
382: 
383: impl<T> UnwindSafe for Sender<T> {}
384: impl<T> RefUnwindSafe for Sender<T> {}
385: 
386: impl<T> Sender<T> {
387:     /// Attempts to send a message into the channel without blocking.
388:     ///
389:     /// This method will either send a message into the channel immediately or return an error if
390:     /// the channel is full or disconnected. The returned error contains the original message.
391:     ///
392:     /// If called on a zero-capacity channel, this method will send the message only if there
393:     /// happens to be a receive operation on the other side of the channel at the same time.
394:     ///
395:     /// # Examples
396:     ///
397:     /// ```
398:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, TrySendError};
399:     ///
400:     /// let (s, r) = bounded(1);
401:     ///
402:     /// assert_eq!(s.try_send(1), Ok(()));
403:     /// assert_eq!(s.try_send(2), Err(TrySendError::Full(2)));
404:     ///
405:     /// drop(r);
406:     /// assert_eq!(s.try_send(3), Err(TrySendError::Disconnected(3)));
407:     /// ```
408:     pub fn try_send(&self, msg: T) -> Result<(), TrySendError<T>> {
409:         match &self.flavor {
410:             SenderFlavor::Array(chan) => chan.try_send(msg),
411:             SenderFlavor::List(chan) => chan.try_send(msg),
412:             SenderFlavor::Zero(chan) => chan.try_send(msg),
413:         }
414:     }
415: 
416:     /// Blocks the current thread until a message is sent or the channel is disconnected.
417:     ///
418:     /// If the channel is full and not disconnected, this call will block until the send operation
419:     /// can proceed. If the channel becomes disconnected, this call will wake up and return an
420:     /// error. The returned error contains the original message.
421:     ///
422:     /// If called on a zero-capacity channel, this method will wait for a receive operation to
423:     /// appear on the other side of the channel.
424:     ///
425:     /// # Examples
426:     ///
427:     /// ```
428:     /// use std::thread;
429:     /// use std::time::Duration;
430:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, SendError};
431:     ///
432:     /// let (s, r) = bounded(1);
433:     /// assert_eq!(s.send(1), Ok(()));
434:     ///
435:     /// thread::spawn(move || {
436:     ///     assert_eq!(r.recv(), Ok(1));
437:     ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
438:     ///     drop(r);
439:     /// });
440:     ///
441:     /// assert_eq!(s.send(2), Ok(()));
442:     /// assert_eq!(s.send(3), Err(SendError(3)));
443:     /// ```
444:     pub fn send(&self, msg: T) -> Result<(), SendError<T>> {
445:         match &self.flavor {
446:             SenderFlavor::Array(chan) => chan.send(msg, None),
447:             SenderFlavor::List(chan) => chan.send(msg, None),
448:             SenderFlavor::Zero(chan) => chan.send(msg, None),
449:         }
450:         .map_err(|err| match err {
451:             SendTimeoutError::Disconnected(msg) => SendError(msg),
452:             SendTimeoutError::Timeout(_) => unreachable!(),
453:         })
454:     }
455: 
456:     /// Waits for a message to be sent into the channel, but only for a limited time.
457:     ///
458:     /// If the channel is full and not disconnected, this call will block until the send operation
459:     /// can proceed or the operation times out. If the channel becomes disconnected, this call will
460:     /// wake up and return an error. The returned error contains the original message.
461:     ///
462:     /// If called on a zero-capacity channel, this method will wait for a receive operation to
463:     /// appear on the other side of the channel.
464:     ///
465:     /// # Examples
466:     ///
467:     /// ```
468:     /// use std::thread;
469:     /// use std::time::Duration;
470:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, SendTimeoutError};
471:     ///
472:     /// let (s, r) = bounded(0);
473:     ///
474:     /// thread::spawn(move || {
475:     ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
476:     ///     assert_eq!(r.recv(), Ok(2));
477:     ///     drop(r);
478:     /// });
479:     ///
480:     /// assert_eq!(
481:     ///     s.send_timeout(1, Duration::from_millis(500)),
482:     ///     Err(SendTimeoutError::Timeout(1)),
483:     /// );
484:     /// assert_eq!(
485:     ///     s.send_timeout(2, Duration::from_secs(1)),
486:     ///     Ok(()),
487:     /// );
488:     /// assert_eq!(
489:     ///     s.send_timeout(3, Duration::from_millis(500)),
490:     ///     Err(SendTimeoutError::Disconnected(3)),
491:     /// );
492:     /// ```
493:     pub fn send_timeout(&self, msg: T, timeout: Duration) -> Result<(), SendTimeoutError<T>> {
494:         match Instant::now().checked_add(timeout) {
495:             Some(deadline) => self.send_deadline(msg, deadline),
496:             None => self.send(msg).map_err(SendTimeoutError::from),
497:         }
498:     }
499: 
500:     /// Waits for a message to be sent into the channel, but only until a given deadline.
501:     ///
502:     /// If the channel is full and not disconnected, this call will block until the send operation
503:     /// can proceed or the operation times out. If the channel becomes disconnected, this call will
504:     /// wake up and return an error. The returned error contains the original message.
505:     ///
506:     /// If called on a zero-capacity channel, this method will wait for a receive operation to
507:     /// appear on the other side of the channel.
508:     ///
509:     /// # Examples
510:     ///
511:     /// ```
512:     /// use std::thread;
513:     /// use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
514:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, SendTimeoutError};
515:     ///
516:     /// let (s, r) = bounded(0);
517:     ///
518:     /// thread::spawn(move || {
519:     ///     thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1));
520:     ///     assert_eq!(r.recv(), Ok(2));
521:     ///     drop(r);
522:     /// });
523:     ///
524:     /// let now = Instant::now();
525:     ///
526:     /// assert_eq!(
527:     ///     s.send_deadline(1, now + Duration::from_millis(500)),
528:     ///     Err(SendTimeoutError::Timeout(1)),
529:     /// );
530:     /// assert_eq!(
531:     ///     s.send_deadline(2, now + Duration::from_millis(1500)),
532:     ///     Ok(()),
533:     /// );
534:     /// assert_eq!(
535:     ///     s.send_deadline(3, now + Duration::from_millis(2000)),
536:     ///     Err(SendTimeoutError::Disconnected(3)),
537:     /// );
538:     /// ```
539:     pub fn send_deadline(&self, msg: T, deadline: Instant) -> Result<(), SendTimeoutError<T>> {
540:         match &self.flavor {
541:             SenderFlavor::Array(chan) => chan.send(msg, Some(deadline)),
542:             SenderFlavor::List(chan) => chan.send(msg, Some(deadline)),
543:             SenderFlavor::Zero(chan) => chan.send(msg, Some(deadline)),
544:         }
545:     }
546: 
547:     /// Returns `true` if the channel is empty.
548:     ///
549:     /// Note: Zero-capacity channels are always empty.
550:     ///
551:     /// # Examples
552:     ///
553:     /// ```
554:     /// use crossbeam_channel::unbounded;
555:     ///
556:     /// let (s, r) = unbounded();
557:     /// assert!(s.is_empty());
558:     ///
559:     /// s.send(0).unwrap();
560:     /// assert!(!s.is_empty());
561:     /// ```
562:     pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
563:         match &self.flavor {
564:             SenderFlavor::Array(chan) => chan.is_empty(),
565:             SenderFlavor::List(chan) => chan.is_empty(),
566:             SenderFlavor::Zero(chan) => chan.is_empty(),
567:         }
568:     }
569: 
570:     /// Returns `true` if the channel is full.
571:     ///
572:     /// Note: Zero-capacity channels are always full.
573:     ///
574:     /// # Examples
575:     ///
576:     /// ```
577:     /// use crossbeam_channel::bounded;
578:     ///
579:     /// let (s, r) = bounded(1);
580:     ///
581:     /// assert!(!s.is_full());
582:     /// s.send(0).unwrap();
583:     /// assert!(s.is_full());
584:     /// ```
585:     pub fn is_full(&self) -> bool {
586:         match &self.flavor {
587:             SenderFlavor::Array(chan) => chan.is_full(),
588:             SenderFlavor::List(chan) => chan.is_full(),
589:             SenderFlavor::Zero(chan) => chan.is_full(),
590:         }
591:     }

593:     /// Returns the number of messages in the channel.
594:     ///
595:     /// # Examples
596:     ///
597:     /// ```
598:     /// use crossbeam_channel::unbounded;
599:     ///
600:     /// let (s, r) = unbounded();
601:     /// assert_eq!(s.len(), 0);
602:     ///
603:     /// s.send(1).unwrap();
604:     /// s.send(2).unwrap();
605:     /// assert_eq!(s.len(), 2);
606:     /// ```
607:     pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
608:         match &self.flavor {
609:             SenderFlavor::Array(chan) => chan.len(),
610:             SenderFlavor::List(chan) => chan.len(),
611:             SenderFlavor::Zero(chan) => chan.len(),
612:         }
613:     }

615:     /// If the channel is bounded, returns its capacity.
616:     ///

618:     ///
619:     /// ```
620:     /// use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, unbounded};
621:     ///
622:     /// let (s, _) = unbounded::<i32>();
623:     /// assert_eq!(s.capacity(), None);
624:     ///
625:     /// let (s, _) = bounded::<i32>(5);
626:     /// assert_eq!(s.capacity(), Some(5));
627:     ///
628:     /// let (s, _) = bounded::<i32>(0);
629:     /// assert_eq!(s.capacity(), Some(0));
630:     /// ```
631:     pub fn capacity(&self) -> Option<usize> {
632:         match &self.flavor {
633:             SenderFlavor::Array(chan) => chan.capacity(),
634:             SenderFlavor::List(chan) => chan.capacity(),
635:             SenderFlavor::Zero(chan) => chan.capacity(),
636:         }
637:     }

639:     /// Returns `true` if senders belong to the same channel.
640:     ///
641:     /// # Examples
642:     ///
643:     /// ```rust
644:     /// use crossbeam_channel::unbounded;
645:     ///
646:     /// let (s, _) = unbounded::<usize>();
647:     ///
648:     /// let s2 = s.clone();
649:     /// assert!(s.same_channel(&s2));
650:     ///
651:     /// let (s3, _) = unbounded();
652:     /// assert!(!s.same_channel(&s3));
653:     /// ```
654:     pub fn same_channel(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
655:         match (&self.flavor, &other.flavor) {
656:             (SenderFlavor::Array(ref a), SenderFlavor::Array(ref b)) => a == b,
657:             (SenderFlavor::List(ref a), SenderFlavor::List(ref b)) => a == b,
658:             (SenderFlavor::Zero(ref a), SenderFlavor::Zero(ref b)) => a == b,
659:             _ => false,
660:         }
661:     }
662: }
